Title: Three AP2/ERF family members modulate flavonoid synthesis by regulating type IV chalcone isomerase in citrus

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Ougan (Citrus reticulata cv. Suavissima); Jincheng (Citrus sinensis (L.) Osbeck)
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CHI-fold protein; type IV CHI subfamily
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Flavanone and flavone branch; early biosynthetic steps of citrus flavanones/flavones
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Early pathway auxiliary step; assists chalcone cyclization and promotes CHS step within metabolon
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Assists stereospecific cyclization of naringenin chalcone to predominantly (2S)-naringenin at high protein concentration; also enhances CHS catalytic efficiency through complex formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor and enhances flux toward target products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Mainly fruit peel; also flowers and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in Ougan peel at early fruit development stage S1 and declines during development; higher in flowers than roots, stems and leaves; expression correlates with flavonoid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in citrus peel and flowers; low in Ougan leaves before transient assays
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage; transient overexpression in peel/leaves; VIGS in seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of citrus flavanones and flavones; overexpression increases flavonoid content, while silencing reduces total CFLs by about 43%
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant protein assay; chiral HPLC/MS; Y2H; BiFC; LCI; kinetic enzyme assay; citrus transient overexpression; Arabidopsis overexpression; citrus VIGS; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CitCHS1; CitCHS2; CitCHI; AtCHS; putative FNS or flavonoid hydroxylases (proposed)
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key flux-enhancing control point within flavonoid metabolon; not established as sole rate-limiting enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CitCHIL1 is the major highly expressed citrus type IV CHI-fold gene whose expression tracks flavonoid accumulation. 2. Recombinant CitCHIL1 assists conversion of naringenin chalcone to predominantly (2S)-naringenin. 3. CitCHIL1 physically interacts with CitCHS1, CitCHS2 and CitCHI and increases CHS catalytic efficiency mainly by raising kcat. 4. Overexpression in citrus and Arabidopsis increases flavonoids, whereas VIGS in citrus decreases total flavonoids.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to enhance citrus flavanone/flavone content and can be stacked with upstream structural genes or regulators for metabolic engineering
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S in pBI121 for overexpression; native CitCHIL1 promoter used in reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of Ougan peel at S1, S3, S5, S7; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although classified as type IV CHI and lacking canonical catalytic residues, it shows weak but stereospecific catalytic assistance at high protein concentration and functions as a metabolon component rather than a typical highly active CHI enzyme.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2/ERF superfamily; DREB A-2 subfamily; AP2 DNA-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Flavonoid biosynthesis via activation of CitCHIL1 transcription
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation by direct promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates CitCHIL1 promoter by directly binding CGCCGC motif (P2)
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: No synergistic complex effect established with CitRAV1 or CitERF33 on CitCHIL1 activation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive activator of CitCHIL1 and flavonoid accumulation, but weaker decisive role than CitERF33-CitRAV1 complex
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in flower and young leaf than in fruit; expression correlated with CitCHIL1 by transcriptome screening
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transient overexpression in Ougan leaves increased CitCHIL1 transcript and flavonoid content
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit development; transient overexpression in citrus leaves
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a positive regulator of flavonoid biosynthesis by activating CitCHIL1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq correlation analysis; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; subcellular localization; citrus transient overexpression; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator to raise citrus flavonoid levels, though less potent than CitERF33/CitRAV1 coexpression
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of Ougan peel at S1, S3, S5, S7; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CitERF32 was identified from coexpression and promoter prediction analyses as a CitCHIL1 regulator. 2. It activates the CitCHIL1 promoter in dual-luciferase assays. 3. EMSA showed direct binding to the CGCCGC motif in the CitCHIL1 promoter. 4. Transient overexpression in citrus leaves increased CitCHIL1 expression and flavonoid content.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A DREB factor homologous to Arabidopsis DREB2-type proteins; possible links to abiotic stress are discussed but not experimentally tested in this paper.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2/ERF superfamily; DREB A-2 subfamily; AP2 DNA-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Flavonoid biosynthesis via activation of CitCHIL1 transcription
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation; also acts in transcription complex with CitRAV1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates CitCHIL1 by binding the CGCCGC motif in its promoter; activation is strongly enhanced by CitRAV1 interaction
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms transcription complex with CitRAV1; also may homodimerize/associate in nucleus
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive regulator because it both directly binds the promoter and mediates strong synergistic activation with CitRAV1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transient overexpression in Ougan leaves increased CitCHIL1 and flavonoid content; coexpression with CitRAV1 gave stronger effect
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit development; transient overexpression/coexpression in citrus leaves
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates citrus flavonoid biosynthesis, with stronger effect when paired with CitRAV1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq correlation analysis; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; pull-down; BiFC; subcellular localization; citrus transient overexpression/coexpression; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong engineering target, especially in combination with CitRAV1, to boost citrus flavonoid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of Ougan peel at S1, S3, S5, S7; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CitERF33 activates the CitCHIL1 promoter and directly binds the CGCCGC motif. 2. Its expression pattern resembles CitCHIL1. 3. CitERF33 physically interacts with CitRAV1 to form a nuclear transcription complex. 4. Coexpression with CitRAV1 strongly increases CitCHIL1 expression and flavonoid accumulation beyond individual factors.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents the DREB component of a newly described DREB-RAV transcription complex controlling citrus flavonoid production.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2/ERF superfamily; RAV family; AP2 domain and B3-like domain
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Flavonoid biosynthesis via indirect activation of CitCHIL1 transcription
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Indirect transcriptional activation through protein complex formation with CitERF33
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Enhances CitERF33-mediated activation of the CitCHIL1 promoter; no direct DNA binding to tested CitCHIL1 promoter motif detected
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms homodimers/oligomers and a transcription complex with CitERF33 in nucleus
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent enhancer that greatly boosts CitCHIL1 activation when combined with CitERF33
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Mainly expressed in fruit and mature leaves; slight increase from S2 to S6 in fruit
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transient overexpression in Ougan leaves increased CitCHIL1 and flavonoid content; coexpression with CitERF33 gave strongest effect
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit development; transient overexpression/coexpression in citrus leaves
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of flavonoid accumulation mainly by forming a synergistic transcription complex with CitERF33
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq correlation analysis; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA negative for direct binding; pull-down; BiFC; subcellular localization; citrus transient overexpression/coexpression; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful in combinatorial engineering with CitERF33 to strongly enhance citrus flavonoid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of Ougan peel at S1, S3, S5, S7; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CitRAV1 activates the CitCHIL1 promoter in vivo but does not directly bind the tested promoter motif in EMSA. 2. CitRAV1 interacts with CitERF33 in vitro and in vivo, and can also self-associate. 3. CitRAV1-CitERF33 coexpression causes strong synergistic activation of CitCHIL1. 4. Coexpression increases citrus leaf flavonoid content more than either factor alone.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Defines a new DREB-RAV transcription complex in citrus flavonoid regulation.
